_comment: Test fixtures for the Read tool (execute_read). Error messages are sourced from tools/__init__.py:execute_read. offset is 1-indexed; limit caps the number of lines returned.

cases.description: Reading an existing plain-text file returns its full content without error.
cases.setup:
Create file at tests/fixtures/sample_data/hello.txt with content:
  Line 1: Hello, Charon!
  Line 2: This is a fixture.
  Line 3: End of file.

cases.description: Reading a path that does not exist must return an error with 'not found' in the message.
Reading with offset=3 and limit=2 returns only lines 3–4 of the file, plus pagination metadata.
cases.setup:
Create file at tests/fixtures/sample_data/numbered.txt with 10 lines:
  line 1
  line 2
  line 3
  line 4
  line 5
  line 6
  line 7
  line 8
  line 9
  line 10

cases.description: Passing an empty string as path must return an error because path is required.
Passing a path that resolves to a directory (not a regular file) must return an error.
cases.setup: Ensure directory tests/fixtures/sample_data exists (created by other cases).

cases.description: Reading a UTF-8 file that contains multi-byte unicode characters (CJK, emoji, accented letters) returns them intact.
cases.setup:
Create file at tests/fixtures/sample_data/unicode.txt with content:
  Héllo Wörld
  日本語テスト
  Emoji: 🚀🌍
  Arabic: مرحبا
  Greek: Ελληνικά

cases.description: Using an offset larger than the total line count returns an empty body but no error, with pagination metadata referencing the real line count.
cases.setup:
Create file at tests/fixtures/sample_data/short.txt with exactly 3 lines:
  alpha
  beta
  gamma

cases.description: Passing an absolute (rather than relative) path resolves correctly and returns file content.
cases.setup: Create /tmp/charon_fixture_absolute_test.txt with content: absolute path content
